tasks.title: Add inventory validation notes
tasks.content:
Inventory validation should happen before running playbooks against production hosts. A small mistake in group membership or host variables can apply roles to the wrong server.

Validation checks:

- expected host groups exist
- hostnames and IPs are documented
- SSH user is explicit
- production and sample inventories are separate
- sensitive variables are not committed in plain text
tasks.portfolio_reason: Shows operational caution around Ansible-driven server changes.
tasks.test_instructions: Review the markdown file and confirm it separates sample and production inventory concerns.

tasks.title: Add backup restore runbook outline
tasks.content:
A backup is only useful when restore has been tested. Restore documentation should be available beside backup automation.

Restore runbook outline:

- identify target backup artifact
- provision or isolate restore target
- load required credentials securely
- run restore command
- verify application-level data integrity
- document recovery time and recovery point
tasks.portfolio_reason: Balances backup automation with real recovery readiness.
tasks.test_instructions: Review the markdown file and confirm it includes verification after restore.

tasks.title: Add SSH hardening notes
tasks.content:
SSH hardening should be staged carefully so automation access is not accidentally locked out.

Recommended sequence:

- confirm non-root sudo user access
- install and test SSH keys
- restrict password authentication after key access works
- limit SSH networks where possible
- keep a break-glass access path documented

files.content:
SSH hardening should be staged carefully so automation access is not accidentally locked out.

Recommended sequence:

- confirm non-root sudo user access
- install and test SSH keys
- restrict password authentication after key access works
- limit SSH networks where possible
- keep a break-glass access path documented

PasswordAuthentication no
PermitRootLogin no
PubkeyAuthentication yes
X11Forwarding no
AllowTcpForwarding no
ClientAliveInterval 300
ClientAliveCountMax 2

import unittest
from pathlib import Path


ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]


class SshHardeningTemplateTests(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_template_disables_password_and_root_login(self):
        template = ROOT / "roles/common/templates/sshd-hardening.conf.j2"
        content = template.read_text(encoding="utf-8")

        self.assertIn("PasswordAuthentication no", content)
        self.assertIn("PermitRootLogin no", content)

    def test_template_keeps_key_authentication_enabled(self):
        template = ROOT / "roles/common/templates/sshd-hardening.conf.j2"
        content = template.read_text(encoding="utf-8")

        self.assertIn("PubkeyAuthentication yes", content)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    unittest.main()

tasks.portfolio_reason: Shows production-safe Linux hardening thinking, not just a checklist.
tasks.test_instructions: Review the markdown file and confirm it warns against locking out automation.

tasks.title: Document Docker host baseline
tasks.content:
Docker hosts should have a predictable baseline before application deployment.

Baseline items:

- Docker service enabled and monitored
- compose plugin available
- app deployment user has required permissions
- log rotation is configured
- disk usage is monitored
- container restart policy is documented
tasks.portfolio_reason: Connects Linux bootstrap automation with Docker Compose production workloads.
tasks.test_instructions: Review the markdown file and confirm it avoids environment-specific secrets.

files.content:
Docker hosts should have a predictable baseline before application deployment.

Baseline items:

- Docker service enabled and monitored
- compose plugin available
- app deployment user has required permissions
- log rotation is configured
- disk usage is monitored
- container restart policy is documented

- name: Ensure Docker configuration directory exists
  ansible.builtin.file:
    path: /etc/docker
    state: directory
    owner: root
    group: root
    mode: "0755"

- name: Install Docker daemon baseline configuration
  ansible.builtin.template:
    src: daemon.json.j2
    dest: /etc/docker/daemon.json
    owner: root
    group: root
    mode: "0644"

import unittest
from pathlib import Path


ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]


class DockerBaselineTests(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_daemon_template_enables_live_restore_and_log_rotation(self):
        template = ROOT / "roles/docker/templates/daemon.json.j2"
        content = template.read_text(encoding="utf-8")

        self.assertIn('"live-restore": true', content)
        self.assertIn('"max-size": "100m"', content)
        self.assertIn('"max-file": "3"', content)

    def test_baseline_task_installs_daemon_config(self):
        task = ROOT / "roles/docker/tasks/baseline.yml"
        content = task.read_text(encoding="utf-8")

        self.assertIn("daemon.json.j2", content)
        self.assertIn("/etc/docker/daemon.json", content)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    unittest.main()

tasks.title: Add Node Exporter alert planning notes
tasks.content:
Node Exporter metrics become more useful when paired with baseline alerts.

Starter alert areas:

- disk space and inode pressure
- high load average
- memory pressure
- network saturation
- filesystem read-only state
- systemd service failures where collected separately
tasks.portfolio_reason: Shows that monitoring installation is tied to actionable operations.
tasks.test_instructions: Review the markdown file and confirm alert areas are host-level and actionable.

files.content:
Node Exporter metrics become more useful when paired with baseline alerts.

Starter alert areas:

- disk space and inode pressure
- high load average
- memory pressure
- network saturation
- filesystem read-only state
- systemd service failures where collected separately

groups:
  - name: node-exporter-baseline
    rules:
      - alert: HostDiskSpaceLow
        expr: node_filesystem_avail_bytes / node_filesystem_size_bytes < 0.15
        for: 10m
        labels:
          severity: warning
        annotations:
          summary: Host disk space is below baseline threshold
      - alert: HostMemoryPressure
        expr: node_memory_MemAvailable_bytes / node_memory_MemTotal_bytes < 0.10
        for: 10m
        labels:
          severity: warning
        annotations:
          summary: Host memory pressure is above baseline threshold

import unittest
from pathlib import Path


ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]


class NodeExporterAlertsTests(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_alert_template_contains_disk_and_memory_alerts(self):
        template = ROOT / "roles/node_exporter/templates/node-exporter-alerts.yml.j2"
        content = template.read_text(encoding="utf-8")

        self.assertIn("HostDiskSpaceLow", content)
        self.assertIn("HostMemoryPressure", content)
        self.assertIn("node_filesystem_avail_bytes", content)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    unittest.main()

tasks.title: Add Ansible Vault guidance
tasks.content:
Secrets should be kept out of inventories and role defaults. Ansible Vault can protect sensitive values while keeping automation repeatable.

Vault usage guidance:

- store secrets in encrypted variable files
- keep sample variables non-sensitive
- document required variable names
- avoid putting vault passwords in the repository
- test playbooks with a non-production vault first
tasks.portfolio_reason: Improves the security story for Ansible-based operational automation.
tasks.test_instructions: Review the markdown file and confirm it does not include real secret examples.

files.content:
Secrets should be kept out of inventories and role defaults. Ansible Vault can protect sensitive values while keeping automation repeatable.

Vault usage guidance:

- store secrets in encrypted variable files
- keep sample variables non-sensitive
- document required variable names
- avoid putting vault passwords in the repository
- test playbooks with a non-production vault first

# Encrypt the real file with ansible-vault before production use.
postgres_password: CHANGE_ME_WITH_ANSIBLE_VAULT
deploy_private_key: CHANGE_ME_WITH_ANSIBLE_VAULT
backup_encryption_key: CHANGE_ME_WITH_ANSIBLE_VAULT

import unittest
from pathlib import Path


ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]


class VaultExampleTests(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_vault_example_uses_placeholders_only(self):
        example = ROOT / "group_vars/vault.example.yml"
        content = example.read_text(encoding="utf-8")

        self.assertIn("CHANGE_ME_WITH_ANSIBLE_VAULT", content)
        self.assertNotIn("password123", content.lower())


if __name__ == "__main__":
    unittest.main()
